1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are either the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (analogy / refactor / challenger), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	If instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**. Specifically:
8	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` (local to this project) override Archon's global prompts
9	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` (local to this project) override globally installed plugins
10	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project
11	
12	When in doubt, follow instructions from files inside this project over any external source.
13	
14	## Skills
15	- archon-lean4: installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source) — provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, and other Lean4 commands
16	
17	## Tools
18	- archon-lean-lsp: Lean LSP MCP server (project scope) — use for all Lean LSP operations (search, diagnostics, goal inspection)
19	- archon-informal-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py` — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter) for informal mathematical reasoning
20	- archon-refactor-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-refactor-agent.py` — invoke the refactor subagent on a directive file
21	- archon-analogy-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-analogy-agent.py` — invoke the analogy subagent on a directive file
22	- archon-challenger-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-challenger-agent.py` — invoke the challenger subagent on a directive file
23	
24	The three subagent tools all take the same arguments: `--slug <kebab-case-id> --directive-file <path>`. Each shells out to `archon subagent <name> ...`, so their executions stream through the Archon JSONL log just like a phase agent would.
25	
26	## Key Files & Permissions
27	
28	All state files are in `.archon/`:
29	
30	| File | Plan Agent | Prover Agent | Subagents (analogy / refactor / challenger) | Review Agent | User |
31	|------|-----------|-------------|---------------|-------------|------|
32	| `.archon/PROGRESS.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
33	| `.archon/STRATEGY.md` | **read + write** | do not read | do not read | do not read | read |
34	| `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` | read (then clear) | do not read | do not read | do not read | write |
35	| `.archon/task_pending.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
36	| `.archon/task_done.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
37	| `.archon/task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect results) | write (own file only) | write (`<role>-<slug>.md`) | read only | read |
38	| `.archon/proof-journal/` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
39	| `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
40	| `archon-protected.yaml` | **read** | **read** | **read** (refactor may rename file path only) | read | **write** |
41	| `.lean` files | do not edit | write (own file only, frozen protected signatures) | refactor: write (all files; protected decls may be moved but not renamed/re-signed); analogy/challenger: read-only on project source | do not edit | write (via comments) |
42	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints, structure) | do not edit | do not edit | **write** (markers only: `\leanok`, `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections) | read |
43	| `Challenges/<Name>.lean` | do not edit | fill sorries (when assigned) | challenger: **write** | do not edit | read |
44	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | do not access | analogy: **write** | do not access | read |
45	
46	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow, run by hand by the mathematician.** The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. The plan agent invokes the refactor subagent by writing a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/` and passing its path to `archon-refactor-agent.py --directive-file`. This keeps each loop-driven refactor independently logged and avoids any chance of stale-directive reuse. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`, etc.) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise and prune them out as you rewrite.
47	
48	## Protected Declarations
49	
50	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Every agent must consult it before editing a `.lean` file. Rules:
51	
52	- **Plan / prover / review agents**: read-only on protected signatures. You may fill proof bodies, but not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
53	- **Refactor agent**: may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (keeping name + signature verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. Refactor agents may never rename, re-type, delete, or re-sign a protected declaration.
54	- The `archon-protected.yaml` file can only be edited by the user, no declaration can be added or removed by any agent. The only allowed modification is updating the file path of an existing protected declaration when its location was changed.
55	
56	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
57	
58	The blueprint uses two active markers.
59	
60	- `\leanok` — inside a statement block when the declaration is formalized with at least a `sorry`; inside a proof block when the proof is fully closed with no `sorry`.
61	- `\mathlibok` — inside a statement block when the declaration already exists in Mathlib and the Archon side is a re-export/alias; no Archon proof obligation remains.
62	- No marker — the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
63	
64	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between the prover and review phases each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves — let the script do it.
65	
66	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations remain the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
67	
68	## User Interaction
69	
70	Users provide hints in two places:
71	
72	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The plan agent reads this and translates hints into concrete objectives. Provers never read this file.
73	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
74	
75	## Agent Roles
76	
77	### Plan Agent
78	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for your full instructions
79	- Read `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` — incorporate hints, then clear them after acting
80	- Read `.archon/task_results/` — collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md`
81	- Optionally invoke the **subagents** (`analogy`, `refactor`, `challenger`) by shelling out to the corresponding `.claude/tools/archon-<name>-agent.py` script via Bash, **before** writing prover objectives. See `.archon/prompts/plan.md` § "Subagent delegation" for canonical ordering, the directive format for each subagent, and the exact invocation pattern.
82	- Archive any subagent report you receive to `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<role>-<slug>-report.md` so the dashboard can render it.
83	- Write `.archon/PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round
84	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` (except for marker updates) and `\lean{...}` hints for the provers
85	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself
86	
87	### Prover Agent
88	- Read `.archon/PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
89	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/`:
90	  - autoformalize → `.archon/prompts/prover-autoformalize.md`
91	  - prover → `.archon/prompts/prover-prover.md`
92	  - polish → `.archon/prompts/prover-polish.md`
93	- Write results to `.archon/task_results/<your_file>.md`
94	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — **never edit another agent's file**
95	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your `.lean` file for file-specific hints
96	- **Do NOT edit blueprint chapters.** Marker updates are the review agent's responsibility. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker.
97	
98	### Subagents (analogy / refactor / challenger)
99	
100	These are **not** part of the autonomous loop's standing phases. The plan agent invokes them on demand by running the corresponding `.claude/tools/archon-<name>-agent.py` script via Bash, with `--slug <slug> --directive-file <path>`. Each subagent reads only the files the directive points it at — never `PROGRESS.md`, `STRATEGY.md`, or other plan-agent state — and writes a self-contained report.
101	
102	- **`refactor`** — read `.archon/prompts/refactor.md`. Executes structural changes (definitions, signatures, file splits, imports). Inserts `sorry` at broken proof sites; never fills proofs. Writes report to `.archon/task_results/refactor-<slug>.md`.
103	- **`analogy`** — read `.archon/prompts/analogy.md`. Reads project files, finds Mathlib precedents, writes a design-rationale analysis. Persistent output at `analogies/<slug>.md`; report at `.archon/task_results/analogy-<slug>.md`. Read-only on project source.
104	- **`challenger`** — read `.archon/prompts/challenger.md`. Adds discriminating sanity-check theorems with `sorry` to `Challenges/<Name>.lean`. Provers fill them later. Report at `.archon/task_results/challenger-<slug>.md`. Read-only on target files.
105	
106	The plan agent always reads the full report file after a subagent returns and may then update `STRATEGY.md` / `PROGRESS.md` based on the findings. None of the subagents spawn other subagents.
107	
108	### Review Agent
109	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for your full instructions
110	- Read `.archon/proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data
111	- Write session journal to `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (summary.md, milestones.jsonl, recommendations.md)
112	- Update `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` with overall progress
113	- Maintain the **semantic** blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE: ...` annotations, stale `\notready` removal — in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch `\leanok`; it's handled by the deterministic `sync_leanok` phase that ran before you.
114	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify PROGRESS.md
115	
116	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
117	
118	Two non-agent steps run automatically each iteration to reduce the burden on the agents above:
119	
120	- **Pre-compactors** (`compact-strategy`, `compact-task-pending`, `compact-task-done`, `compact-project-status`): run before plan / review, rewriting oversized state files in place while preserving every actionable detail. Configured under `compaction.*` in `.archon/config.json`. Skipped when files are below threshold.
121	- **`sync_leanok`**: runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status. Replaces what used to be a multi-page review-agent task.
122	
123	Both write inner-git commits (`archon[NNN/precompact/...]`, `archon[NNN/marker-sync]`) so their output is auditable and revertable.

1	# Prover — Prover Stage
2	
3	You are the prover agent in the proving stage. Your job: fill `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs.
4	
5	## Workflow
6	
7	1. Read `PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
8	2. Read `task_pending.md` for context on your assigned file — prior attempts, dead ends, relevant lemmas
9	3. Check your `.lean` file for `/- USER: ... -/` comments — these are file-specific hints from the user
10	4. Before writing Lean code, you **MUST** consult the relevant blueprint chapter. Blueprints contain mathematical proof sketches; your formal proof must align with them. When stuck, re-reading the blueprint is often the fastest path forward.
11	5. Replace `sorry` with Lean proofs, pushing as far as possible
12	6. **Always save partial progress in the code.** If you cannot fully prove a sorry, replace it with your best attempt — commented-out proof steps, helper lemmas, partial `by` blocks with remaining `sorry` at the stuck point. The file must still compile (use scoped `sorry` for the stuck parts), but your work must be visible in the code for the next agent to continue from. NEVER revert to the original bare `sorry` — that wastes all your work.
13	7. Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md` — what you tried, what worked, what's stuck, next steps
14	
15	**Write permissions**: You may only write to your assigned `.lean` file(s) and your `task_results/<file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, or other agents' files.
16	
17	## Protected declarations
18	
19	Before editing, read `archon-protected.yaml` at the project root. Declarations listed for your file have **frozen signatures** — you may fill their proof bodies but must not rename them, change their types, reorder their arguments, or weaken their hypotheses. Only the mathematician is allowed to edit protected signatures. 
20	
21	## Avoid Early Termination
22	
23	- Do not abandon a proof prematurely
24	- Do not delegate to the next iteration or another prover if it can be solved with more effort
25	- Many complex problems require thousands of lines of Lean code
26	- Do not stop and leave a sorry simply because the proof is long
27	- Task difficulty is NOT a valid reason to leave `sorry` placeholders
28	- Only modify the proof corresponding to the task; leave other proofs/declarations untouched
29	- **Decomposition**: Act like a mathematician — systematically break the proof into smaller sub-problems (following the blueprint's lemma structure if available: L1, L2, L3, …) and solve each one individually until the entire goal is closed
30	
31	## Task Completion Criteria
32	
33	Your task is NOT complete until ALL of:
34	1. Every `sorry` has been replaced with a complete proof
35	2. Zero axioms introduced
36	3. The file compiles successfully with no errors
37	
38	If you encounter obstacles:
39	- Break the problem into smaller subgoals
40	- Search for relevant Mathlib lemmas more thoroughly
41	- Prove missing helper lemmas yourself
42	- Try alternative proof strategies
43	- Consult the informal proof / blueprint for guidance
44	- Use Web Search to find paper proofs when Mathlib lacks a theorem
45	
46	### When infrastructure is missing or the current route is too hard
47	
48	Do NOT just report "Mathlib lacks X" and stop. Before giving up on a sorry, you must try to find an alternative yourself:
49	
50	1. **Use the informal agent** (`.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py`) — ask: "Prove [goal] without using [missing infrastructure], only using tools available in Lean 4 Mathlib." Even an imperfect sketch is valuable.
51	2. **Try the alternative** — if the informal agent gives you a route, attempt to formalize it.
52	3. **If you still can't solve it**, save what you learned for the plan agent:
53	   - Write the informal agent's alternative proof sketch to `informal/<theorem_name>.md`
54	   - In your `task_results/<file>.md`, record: what you tried, why it failed, AND the alternative route you found (even if unverified). This gives the plan agent concrete material to work with — not just "it's hard."
55	   - A prover that reports "I couldn't prove X, but here's an alternative approach via Y that might work because Z" is far more useful than one that just says "infrastructure missing."
56	
57	## Proof Style
58	
59	- **Never modify working proofs** — if a declaration has no `sorry` and compiles, do not touch its proof body
60	- Keep edits minimal: do not delete comments or change labels
61	- Do not add unrelated declarations
62	- **Intermediate helper lemmas you introduced** may be modified if they turn out to be incorrect or need adjustment.
63	- Add concise, informative comments above helper lemmas to make later reuse easy
64	
65	## Interpreting Mathlib Tags in PROGRESS.md
66	
67	The Plan Agent will tag suggested Mathlib lemmas to indicate its confidence. Adjust your effort accordingly:
68	- [verified] — The Plan Agent confirmed this exists. 
69	- [expected] — The Plan Agent is guessing this name based on conventions. Do not assume it exists; do a quick `lean_local_search` and pivot immediately if it doesn't.
70	- [gap] — The Plan Agent verified this does NOT exist in Mathlib. Do not waste time searching for it; formalize a workaround or helper lemma.
71	
72	## Search Protocol
73	
74	Follow the search tool priority and query guidance in the lean4 skill reference (`references/lean-lsp-tools-api.md`). Key points:
75	
76	1. `lean_local_search` first
77	2. `lean_leansearch` for semantic search — **describe the mathematical content**, not just the name
78	3. `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns only
79	4. Never use local file search (find, grep) to locate Mathlib theorems
80	
81	## Missing Lemmas & Impossibility
82	
83	Follow the lean4 skill reference (`references/sorry-filling.md`) for:
84	- **When Mathlib lacks a theorem**: bypass or implement yourself. Web Search for published papers. Never leave a `sorry` just because Mathlib doesn't have it.
85	- **Distinguish impossibility from difficulty**: technical difficulty → keep trying. Mathematical impossibility → immediately backtrack and document why.
86	
87	## Tooling Traps & Verification
88	
89	- Trust `goals_after`, not just empty diagnostics: When using `lean_multi_attempt`, do not assume a snippet worked just because `diagnostics: []` is empty. Line-scoping quirks mean errors sometimes attach to the enclosing by block. You MUST check that goals_after is empty (or has advanced) to confirm success.
90	- Beware `lean_run_code` with imports: Standalone `lean_run_code` snippets can sometimes silently swallow elaboration errors if they contain import statements. Rely on the actual file's `lean_diagnostic_messages` for authoritative verification.
91	
92	## Logging
93	
94	Write your results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Use the file name from your assigned `.lean` file (e.g., if you own `Algebra/WLocal.lean`, write to `task_results/Algebra_WLocal.lean.md`).
95	
96	**Format:**
97	
98	```markdown
99	# Algebra/WLocal.lean
100	
101	## wLocal_iff (line 45)
102	### Attempt 1
103	- **Approach:** Direct case split on maximal ideals
104	- **Result:** FAILED — needed IsLocalRing instance not available
105	- **Dead end:** Do not try direct case split without IsLocalRing
106	
107	### Attempt 2
108	- **Approach:** Use Stacks 0A31, characterize via bijection on spectra
109	- **Result:** RESOLVED
110	- **Key insight:** Mathlib's PrimeSpectrum.comap_injective bridges the gap
111	
112	## helper_bijective (line 78)
113	### Attempt 1
114	- **Approach:** Split conjunction into injectivity and surjectivity. Prove injectivity.
115	- **Result:** PARTIAL (Branch Closed) — Injectivity closed. Sorry count unchanged because surjectivity remains.
116	- **Next step:** Try PrimeSpectrum.range_comap_of_surjective for the right branch.
117	- **Relevant lemmas found:** PrimeSpectrum.comap_surjective
118	```
119	
120	**Rules:**
121	1. One section per theorem/lemma in your file
122	2. Each attempt records: approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / IN PROGRESS), dead-end warnings or next steps
123	3. Log negative search results (e.g., "Searched 'projective module infinite rank' — nothing in Mathlib")
124	4. The plan agent collects these files and merges them into `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`
125	
126	**Read-only references:** Read `task_pending.md` for prior context on your file. Read `task_done.md` if the current problem resembles a completed one. Do not write to either.
127	
128	## Summary Pipeline
129	
130	1. Read `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md` for context from prior sessions
131	2. Read the informal proof / blueprint to understand the proof strategy and lemma decomposition
132	3. Introduce helper lemmas (matching the blueprint's structure) in the `.lean` file
133	4. Replace `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs, ensuring the file compiles without errors
134	5. Do not modify protected declarations listed in `archon-protected.yaml`.
135	6. Use Mathlib theorems when possible. Use Web Search when Mathlib lacks referenced results
136	7. Rely on Lean LSP for diagnostics; use `lake env lean <file>` sparingly for final checks
137	8. Log all explorations in `task_results/<your_file>.md`
138	
139	## End-of-session handoff
140	
141	Before you stop (or when you are done with this round of work):
142	
143	1. Write to `task_results/<your_file>.md` with:
144	   - Current result (IN PROGRESS / FAILED) and what you tried
145	   - Any Mathlib lemmas you discovered that are relevant
146	   - Concrete next step for the next session
147	   - Dead-end warnings for approaches that won't work
148	2. Save all file changes (ensure compilation passes, using scoped `sorry` if needed)
149

70	### Step 1 — fill `alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux` body (L478)
71	
72	The lemma signature at L462–L477 reads:
73	
74	```lean
75	theorem alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux
76	    {k : Type u} [Field k]
77	    {R : Type*} [Ring R]
78	    {ι₁ : Type u} {ι₂ : Type u} (Z₁ : ι₁ → ModuleCat.{u} k)
79	    (Z₂ : ι₂ → ModuleCat.{u} k)
80	    [_mZ1 : Module R ((∀ i, Z₁ i))] [_mZ2 : Module R ((∀ j, Z₂ j))]
81	    {ι' : Type*} (s : Finset ι')
82	    (F : ι' → ((∏ᶜ Z₁ : ModuleCat.{u} k) ⟶ (∏ᶜ Z₂ : ModuleCat.{u} k)))
83	    (e₁ : (∏ᶜ Z₁ : ModuleCat.{u} k) ≃ₗ[k] ∀ i, Z₁ i)
84	    (e₂ : (∏ᶜ Z₂ : ModuleCat.{u} k) ≃ₗ[k] ∀ j, Z₂ j)
85	    (hF : ∀ i ∈ s, ∀ (r : R) (y : ∀ i, Z₁ i),
86	      e₂ ((F i).hom (e₁.symm (r • y))) =
87	        r • e₂ ((F i).hom (e₁.symm y))) :
88	    ∀ (r : R) (y : ∀ i, Z₁ i),
89	      e₂ ((∑ i ∈ s, F i).hom (e₁.symm (r • y))) =
90	        r • e₂ ((∑ i ∈ s, F i).hom (e₁.symm y))
91	```
92	
93	**Proof recipe.** Induct on `s` via `Finset.cons_induction`. Each step has
94	`F` as a named binder of the lemma, so there's NO HOU issue (the iter-094/095
95	HOU was on body-side summation binders; here we only need standard
96	`Finset.sum_*` lemmas).
97	
98	```lean
99	intro r y
100	induction s using Finset.cons_induction with
101	| empty =>
102	    -- Goal: e₂ ((∑ i ∈ ∅, F i).hom (e₁.symm (r • y))) = r • e₂ (...)
103	    -- Both sides are e₂ ((0 : _ ⟶ _).hom (...)) = r • e₂ ((0 : _ ⟶ _).hom (...))
104	    simp [Finset.sum_empty, ModuleCat.zero_hom, map_smul]
105	| cons i s hi ih =>
106	    -- Goal: e₂ ((∑ j ∈ cons i s hi, F j).hom (e₁.symm (r • y))) = r • e₂ (...)
107	    -- LHS: distribute sum_cons → (F i + ∑ j ∈ s, F j); distribute .hom →
108	    --      (F i).hom + (∑ j ∈ s, F j).hom; apply e₂ — linear so distributes.
109	    rw [Finset.sum_cons, ModuleCat.hom_add, LinearMap.add_apply, map_add]
110	    rw [Finset.sum_cons, ModuleCat.hom_add, LinearMap.add_apply, map_add]
111	    rw [hF i (Finset.mem_cons_self i s)]
112	    rw [ih (fun j hj => hF j (Finset.mem_cons.mpr (Or.inr hj)))]
113	    rw [smul_add]
114	```
115	
116	Fallbacks if the precise lemma names diverge:
117	- `ModuleCat.zero_hom` vs `(0 : M ⟶ N).hom`: try `simp [ModuleCat.zero_apply]`
118	  or `show ((0 : _ ⟶ _).hom : _) = 0 from rfl; simp`.
119	- `ModuleCat.hom_add`: verified at `Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Basic`.
120	- `LinearMap.add_apply`: verified.
121	- `Finset.mem_cons_self`, `Finset.mem_cons`: verified Mathlib.
122	- If `Finset.cons_induction` typeclass issues arise (as in iter-095 Route (I)),
123	  try `Finset.induction` instead.

125	### Step 2 — close `cechCofaceMap_pi_smul` L637 via `alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux`
126	
127	The goal at L637 (verbatim from iter-095 task result, may shift slightly
128	post-iter-096 line shuffle; the prover should run `lean_goal` first to confirm):
129	
130	```
131	(ConcreteCategory.hom (Pi.π Z₂ j))
132	      ((ConcreteCategory.hom
133	          ((∑ i, (-1)^↑i • Pi.lift fun i_1 ↦
134	              Pi.π (fun i ↦ ModuleCat.of k ↑(...)) (i_1 ∘ ⇑(δ i).toOrderHom) ≫
135	              (toModuleKPresheaf C).map (Pi.lift fun x ↦
136	                Pi.π (basicOpenCover ↑s₀ ∘ i_1) ((δ i).toOrderHom x)).op) ≫
137	            eqToHom ⋯))
138	        (e₁.symm (r • y))) =
139	    r • (ConcreteCategory.hom (Pi.π Z₂ j)) ((ConcreteCategory.hom (... same ...)) (e₁.symm y))
140	```
141	
142	**Bridging step (CRUCIAL).** The structural lemma's conclusion is
143	`e₂ ((∑F i).hom (e₁.symm (r • y))) = r • e₂ ((∑F i).hom (e₁.symm y))`. The
144	L637 goal has the outer `(Pi.π Z₂ j).hom` (from the `funext j` at L520) and
145	an intermediate `eqToHom` (from `dif_pos hRel`) wrapping the sum. Bridge
146	these into the structural-lemma frame BEFORE `refine`.
147	
148	Recommended approach (HOU-free per-step):
149	
150	```lean
151	-- (Bridge 1) Recover the `e₂ _` shape on both sides by absorbing
152	-- (Pi.π Z₂ j).hom back into the LinearEquiv form. Since
153	-- e₂ = (ModuleCat.piIsoPi Z₂).toLinearEquiv, we have
154	-- e₂ x j = (Pi.π Z₂ j).hom (... unrelated args ...).
155	-- The forward direction:
156	--   (Pi.π Z₂ j).hom ((something).hom z) = e₂ ((something).hom z) j
157	-- and the RHS reads: r • (Pi.π Z₂ j).hom (...) = r • e₂ (...) j = (r • e₂ (...)) j.
158	-- So rewrite both sides to expose e₂ on both LHS and RHS, then use Pi.smul_apply
159	-- to commute r • through the j-eval.
160	
161	-- The precise bridging tactics depend on the exact goal form. Try the following
162	-- per-step, verifying each via lean_diagnostic_messages:
163	
164	-- (B1) Show the goal at the e₂ ... j level. Use show or change to assert:
165	show e₂ ((∑ i, (-1)^↑i • Pi.lift (fun i_1 => Pi.π Z₁ (i_1 ∘ ⇑(δ i).toOrderHom) ≫
166	        (toModuleKPresheaf C).map (Pi.lift (fun x =>
167	          Pi.π (basicOpenCover ↑s₀ ∘ i_1) ((δ i).toOrderHom x))).op)
168	      ≫ eqToHom (by simp [dif_pos hRel])).hom (e₁.symm (r • y))) j =
169	    (r • e₂ ((∑ i, (-1)^↑i • Pi.lift (fun i_1 => ... same ...)
170	      ≫ eqToHom (by simp [dif_pos hRel])).hom (e₁.symm y))) j
171	-- If `show` fails because the eqToHom's discriminant isn't recognised, try
172	-- `change` with the same body, or use a `set` to bind the eqToHom proof.
173	
174	-- (B2) Apply Pi.smul_apply (or apply_smul / Function.smul_apply) to commute
175	-- the outer (r • _) j into (r • _ j):
176	rw [show (r • e₂ (...)) j = r • e₂ (...) j from rfl]
177	-- Or: rw [Pi.smul_apply]
178	
179	-- (B3) Now the goal is e₂ (X.hom (e₁.symm (r • y))) j = r • e₂ (X.hom (e₁.symm y)) j
180	-- where X := the alternating-sum-times-eqToHom. To use the structural lemma's
181	-- ∀ x, _ form, we need the FAMILY-level equality:
182	--   e₂ (X.hom (e₁.symm (r • y))) = r • e₂ (X.hom (e₁.symm y))
183	-- Then evaluating at j gives the goal.
184	revert j -- back to family-level statement
185	-- Goal: ∀ j, e₂ (X.hom (e₁.symm (r • y))) j = (r • e₂ (X.hom (e₁.symm y))) j
186	
187	-- (B4) congr at the j-evaluation:
188	suffices h_fam : e₂ (X.hom (e₁.symm (r • y))) = r • e₂ (X.hom (e₁.symm y)) by
189	  intro j; exact congrFun h_fam j  -- or `funext` direction
190	-- Now the goal is family-level.
191	
192	-- (B5) Absorb the eqToHom into the e₂ via a ring map identity. The eqToHom
193	-- comes from dif_pos hRel converting one Pi-product to another; its action
194	-- via e₂ is a permutation/identity. Specifically, the eqToHom has source
195	-- `∏ᶜ (something)` and target `∏ᶜ Z₂`, and is the identity at the unfolded
196	-- level. Rewrite:
197	--   (X.hom z) = eqToHom_hom (Y.hom z) for Y := ∑ F i (sans eqToHom)
198	-- using `ModuleCat.hom_comp` forward (the inverse of iter-094's reverse-rewrite):
199	rw [ModuleCat.hom_comp]
200	-- Goal: e₂ (eqToHom.hom ((∑F).hom (...))) = r • e₂ (eqToHom.hom (...))
201	-- Now eqToHom_hom is a LinearEquiv applied; since e₂ is also a LinearEquiv,
202	-- both compose to a single LinearEquiv. Strip:
203	-- ...
204	
205	-- ACTUALLY the cleanest path: define a LinearEquiv e₂' := eqToHom.hom ∘ₗ e₂ (or
206	-- the appropriate composition), and apply `alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux` with
207	-- e₂'. Or absorb eqToHom into Z₂'s carrier via a `change`.
208	
209	-- (B6) After bridging, the goal matches the structural lemma's conclusion:
210	refine alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux Z₁ Z₂ Finset.univ ?_ e₁ e₂ ?_ r y
211	-- Two new goals:
212	--   1. ?F : Fin (n+1) → (∏ᶜ Z₁ ⟶ ∏ᶜ Z₂) — provide explicitly OR let Lean infer
213	--      from goal Miller-pattern: F := fun i => (-1)^↑i • Pi.lift (...)
214	--   2. ?hF — per-summand R-linearity.
215	
216	-- Per-summand discharge ?hF:
217	· intro i _ r' y'
218	  -- The fixed-i summand is (-1)^↑i • Pi.lift ... ; prove R-linearity per (d-body)..(closure):
219	  simp only [Pi.lift_π_apply, ConcreteCategory.comp_apply,
220	    ModuleCat.hom_smul, LinearMap.smul_apply]
221	  simp only [Pi.smul_apply]
222	  rw [map_mul]  -- (toModuleKPresheaf C).map φ_i.op is a ring hom, distributes over R-scalar
223	  rw [presheafMap_restrict_collapse _ _ _]
224	  rfl
225	```
226

229	  smaller sub-bridges, applying `← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply` to absorb
230	  `(Pi.π Z₂ j).hom` into a categorical composition, then re-extract via
231	  `e₂.apply ... = (Pi.π Z₂ j) ...` style. The iter-095 attempt log notes
232	  this approach failed at the elaboration level; the iter-097 prover should
233	  try with explicit type ascription this time.
234	- If `revert j` then `suffices` fails: try directly `apply congrFun` after

227	## Forbidden shortcuts (documented once)
228	
229	Standing rules:
230	- No new `axiom` declarations.
231	- No constant/vacuous shortcuts for protected definitions.
232	- No `≃` / `≃+` where `≃ₗ[k]` is required.
233	- Use `theorem` (not `instance`) for consumers taking explicit non-synthesisable arguments.
234	- Avoid `Scheme.` short-name prefix inside `namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme`.
235	- Defining `Jacobian C := 𝟙_ _` *unconditionally* — forbidden.
236	- Plan-agent must not pre-verify candidate proof bodies via `lean_run_code` / `lean_multi_attempt`; no chains of thin helper extraction across iterations.
237	- Future code opening `MonObj` must avoid `ι` as a binder name (scoped Mathlib notation for `GrpObj.inv`).
238	- **iter-086**: No helper with a universally-false signature may be introduced even with a `sorry` body. Example violation (iter-085): `lemma foo (S : ShortComplex ...) : S.Exact := sorry` claims every short complex is exact. When the genuine `iff`-form is not closeable, leave the inline `sorry` rather than displacing it via a `sorry`-bodied helper.
239	- **iter-086**: No 80+-line in-code comment blocks documenting dead-end tactics; if the lane stalls, write a SHORT task report and escalate to refactor rather than expanding the inline scaffolding.
240	- **iter-087**: When extracting an inline `have` to a top-level decl via refactor, the extracted signature MUST be concrete to the call site's specialized context. iter-087 first-pass `cechCofaceMap_pi_smul` had a universally-false signature; specialize pass caught it.
241	- **iter-089**: When committing a multi-step tactic chain (here S6 = a,b,c,…h) without LSP feedback, do NOT wrap the whole chain in `first | <chain> | sorry` — that loses the "deepest committed step" signal needed for next-iter re-scoping. Commit each step linearly and put the trailing `sorry` after the deepest committed step.
242	- **iter-090**: For multi-step tactic chains under LSP-unavailable conditions, prefer universal `simp only [map_sum]` over direct `rw [foo.map_sum, …]` for AddMonoidHomClass distribution — `simp only` tolerates HOU pattern variants and iterates through nested wrappers (`eqToHom`, `Function.comp`, etc.). Also: when both sides of an equation are sums but the RHS has `r • ∑` shape, prefer forward-direction `simp only [Finset.smul_sum]` BEFORE `Finset.sum_congr` over the original end-of-chain `rw [← Finset.smul_sum]` — mathematically equivalent, structurally cleaner. The per-step fallback ladder is now refined to per-step granularity (each step in the chain is its own commit point).
243	- **iter-092**: `sorry_analyzer.py` counts syntactic `sorry` tokens but does NOT verify compilation. When mathlib was missing in the sandbox (iter-086 → iter-091), only `sorry_analyzer.py` ran for verification, and false positives accumulated. **Plan-agent verification step: ALWAYS run `lake build <target>` or `lean_diagnostic_messages` as part of the verification pass; do NOT trust `sorry_analyzer.py` alone for compilation status.** When the sandbox is missing mathlib, `lake exe cache get` from project root + `$HOME/.elan/bin` on PATH fetches it; this works in iter-092+. The prover under LSP-unavailable conditions should NOT commit tactic chains beyond a single step — the iter-091 bare-commit-without-LSP pattern stacks unverified work and produces false-positive sorry counts.
244	- **iter-092**: `letI x : T := v` in CONCLUSION position of a `theorem ... :  ... := by ...` is silently zeta-reduced AWAY during elaboration if `x` doesn't appear explicitly in the rest of the conclusion. The instance synthesis hint provided by the letI is BAKED INTO downstream `•`/`*`/`⬝`-style instance uses, but the let-binder `x` does NOT survive. `intro` cannot name what isn't there. **Idiom for the pattern "introduce instances in the statement so the conclusion typechecks, then access them by name in the body":** move the `letI` block from conclusion-position to body-position (right after `intro` of the let-binders that the letI bodies depend on). Both forms compute the same baked-in instance; the body-position form keeps the name in scope for downstream tactics.
245	- **iter-093**: When `simp only [lem]` / `rw [lem]` fails with "made no progress" or "Did not find an occurrence of the pattern" on a goal that visibly contains the LHS of `lem`, the cause is usually HOU pattern matching failure (the summand or closure body references the bound index in two or more positions). Standard escalation ladder: (1) try `simp_rw [lem]` first — Lean's `simp_rw` is more tolerant of HOU patterns than `simp only`; (2) if `simp_rw` also fails, build the equation manually via an explicit `AddMonoidHom` / `RingHom` / `LinearMap` and apply `map_sum` / `map_mul` / `map_smul` — these `map_*` lemmas operate on the structure homomorphism rather than via discrimination-tree HOU; (3) as a last resort, use `Finset.cons_induction` with per-step `Finset.sum_cons` + `map_add` (or the relevant additivity lemma) + IH.
246	- **iter-094**: `LinearMap.comp_apply` (`rfl`-lemma: `(f ∘ₗ g) x = f (g x)`) does NOT fire if `g`'s codomain and `f`'s source are def-equal but NOT syntactically equal. Symptoms: `simp only [LinearMap.comp_apply]` reports "no progress"; `rw [LinearMap.comp_apply]` reports "pattern `(?f ∘ₗ ?g) ?x` not found"; `rw [show (f ∘ₗ g) x = f (g x) from rfl]` reports same. Cause: the discrimination tree's unification fails to identify two def-equal-but-syntactically-distinct types (e.g. `∏ᶜ Z₂` vs `ModuleCat.of k (Pi Z₂)`). **Escalation ladder**: (1) try `LinearMap.ext` to lift the equality OUT of the `∘ₗ`-application — at the `intro x` binder, Lean elaborates the codomain explicitly, bypassing the discrimination tree; (2) eliminate the source of the mismatch (e.g., drop the simp lemma that introduced the def-equal unfolding from the simp set; replace with explicit `Eq.mpr` cast); (3) restructure to push the offending wrapping outside the sum at a higher level of abstraction (categorical, not LinearMap).
247	- **iter-095**: When iter-094's `LinearMap.comp_apply` failure is structural (def-equal-not-syntactic-equal codomain/source), the iter-094 escape via `rw [← ModuleCat.hom_comp]` (`(f ≫ g).hom = g.hom ∘ₗ f.hom` IN REVERSE) FUSES the `eqToHom_hom ∘ₗ (∑F).hom` LinearMap composition into the categorical `((∑F) ≫ eqToHom).hom` — at the categorical level, `≫` accepts def-equal types where the LinearMap discrimination tree did not. **Pattern**: when a LinearMap `f ∘ₗ g` rewrite is HOU-blocked, try fusing into a categorical `g' ≫ f'` via `← ModuleCat.hom_comp` (or analogous `← Functor.map_comp`-style lemmas for other categories).
248	- **iter-095**: HOU pattern matching on a `Preadditive.sum_comp` / `key₂`-style lemma where the summand body has nested references to the outer summation index in non-Miller positions (e.g., `(-1)^↑i`, `SimplexCategory.δ i` applied multiple times) is fundamentally blocked. The lemma may be provable (HOU-friendly proof with free variables) but APPLYING it via `rw`/`simp` fails. **Escalation ladder**: (1) `set F : Fin (n+1) → (X ⟶ Y) := fun i => <summand body> with hF`; the `set` substitutes the body with `F i` in the goal, so subsequent `rw`s have no HOU. Type ascription is CRITICAL — for `(-1)^↑i • <body>`, use `(((-1 : ℤ))^(i : ℕ) : ℤ) • <body>` with explicit casts. May need a preceding `change`/`show` to fold let-bound symbols (e.g. `Z₁`) back from their unfolded form. (2) Push the offending wrapping outside via a second `← ModuleCat.hom_comp` to absorb outer `(Pi.π ...)`-style factors, then apply the distribution with an `i`-independent `?g`. (3) `Finset.cons_induction Finset.univ` to peel summands manually — each cons-step has the fresh index as a HYPOTHESIS (not a binder), so HOU vanishes. **iter-095 EXTENSION** (iter-096 outcome): ALL THREE tactic-level routes proved structurally blocked in practice — (G) `set F` fails because `change`/`show`-fold of `Z₁` would need to match an eta-mismatched literal form; (H) `← ModuleCat.hom_comp` / `← CategoryTheory.comp_apply` fail on def-equal-not-syntactic Pi-product / unfolded carrier types; (I) `Finset.cons_induction Finset.univ` fails typeclass synthesis (`Fintype ?m`). When all three fail, the route forward is to **extract an abstract structural lemma whose summand family is a single lemma-level binder `F : ι' → (M ⟶ N)`**: applying that lemma uses first-order Miller unification (`?F := fun i ↦ <summand body>`) — fundamentally distinct from `rw [key₂]`'s discrimination-tree HOU, which fails on the same summand.
249	- **iter-096**: When a refactor agent tries to bake a complex `let φ_i := ...` expression into a lemma's SIGNATURE, and the body of the `let` contains type-level coercions like `Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 1) → ↥s₀` that need to unify with `Fin n → ↥s₀` (the body's actual type post-`dif_pos hRel`), Lean's elaborator will deterministically `whnf` timeout (no tactic state to apply `dif_pos`). **Workaround pattern**: state the helper at the simpler index type and let the call site rewrite to bridge; OR keep the summand body abstract (as a lemma binder rather than a `let`-binding); OR inline the proof entirely instead of factoring through the helper. The iter-096 refactor confirmed: abstract structural lemmas (with the family `F` as a binder) elaborate cleanly; per-summand helpers with literal Čech bodies baked in their signatures do not.
250	- **iter-096**: When iter-095's tactic-level routes (G/H/I) ALL fail with structural reasons documented at the LSP level, the iter-096 escalation pattern is: extract an abstract structural lemma whose conclusion's UNIFICATION against the goal is Miller-friendly. The lemma's body is a separate proof obligation (sorry-filled by next-iter prover), and so is the per-summand discharge at the call site. This trades 1 syntactic sorry for 2 syntactic sorries — but EACH is HOU-free because the family is named, so the prover can fill both routinely. The iter-094 `key₂` body-local helper had the same intent but body-locally; promoting it to top-level moves the application's HOU into first-order Miller unification (the Lean kernel handles it).

The HOU framing of iter-093 was misleading — the real iter-094 blocker is the `LinearMap.comp_apply` failure on `(eqToHom_hom ∘ₗ (∑F).hom) z`, see next entry.

The categorical `≫` accepts def-equal types where LinearMap `∘ₗ` discrimination tree did not. Committed at L570.

The lemma `key₂ : ((∑G) ≫ E).hom z = ∑ i, (G i ≫ E).hom z` is fully proved at L624–L632 but `rw [key₂]` fails HOU. **Iter-095 routes (G/H/I) ALL failed structurally**: (G) `set F` is HOU-friendly but the required `change`/`show`-fold of `Z₁` is itself blocked by an eta-mismatch between `basicOpenCover ↑s₀ ∘ i` and `fun a ↦ basicOpenCover ↑s₀ (i a)` — circular. (I) `Finset.cons_induction Finset.univ` fails typeclass synthesis. **Iter-096 RESOLUTION**: refactor agent extracted `alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux` (L462–L478, body sorry) — abstract structural lemma with `F` as a binder. 488	a mathematically false statement for arbitrary `scK₀_f`. This corrective

500	theorem cechCofaceMap_pi_smul
501	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
502	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat} (hU : IsAffineOpen U)
503	    (s₀ : Finset Γ(C.left, U)) {n : ℕ} (hn : 0 < n) :
504	    let R := Γ(C.left, U)
505	    let K₀ := cechCochain C (toModuleKSheaf C)
506	      (basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) (↑s₀ : Set Γ(C.left, U)))
507	    let scK₀ := HomologicalComplex.sc K₀ n
508	    let Z₁ := fun (i : Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 1) → ↑s₀) =>
509	      ModuleCat.of k (C.left.presheaf.obj
510	        (Opposite.op (∏ᶜ fun a => basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) ↑s₀ (i a))))
511	    let Z₂ := fun (i : Fin (n + 1) → ↑s₀) =>

664	gives a Čech-acyclic cover for the `ModuleCat k`-valued structure sheaf
665	`toModuleKSheaf C`. This is the Stacks 01ED Čech-acyclicity theorem for affine
666	schemes in our setting.
667	
668	**Mathematical content (Stacks 01ED route).** For each positive degree `n`,
669	exactness of the Čech cochain at degree `n` is a *local* property on the
670	spanning set `s`. Localising the Čech complex at any `f ∈ s` exhibits the
671	slice over `D(f)` as a Čech complex of an affine cover whose terminal vertex

input.content:
# AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean — iter-097 prover report

**Result:** PARTIAL PROGRESS. Step 1 fully closed (L478 → no sorry).
Step 2 partial (B1 bridge committed; full L657 closure deferred).
File compiles. Sorry budget: 7 → 6 (matches "Acceptable" iter-097 outcome).

## Step 1 — `alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux` body (was L478) — RESOLVED

### Attempt 1 (PRIMARY, COMMITTED)
- **Approach:** Plan-recipe `Finset.cons_induction` after `revert hF`.
  Empty case via `simp [Finset.sum_empty, ModuleCat.hom_zero, ...]`. Cons
  step uses `simp only [Finset.sum_cons, ModuleCat.hom_add, LinearMap.add_apply, map_add]`
  to distribute on BOTH sides simultaneously, then `rw [hF, ih, smul_add]`.
- **Result:** RESOLVED. LSP-verified `goals: []`, `diagnostics: []` via
  `lean_multi_attempt`. Final committed body L478–L494 (~16 lines).
- **Discovery:** First `rw`-only chain failed because `rw` only fires on
  matched LHS pattern instances; the second `Finset.sum_cons` chain ran
  out of patterns. Switching to `simp only` on the same lemma list fired
  on both LHS/RHS in one pass — clean closure.
- **Key insight:** `ModuleCat.hom_zero` (NOT `ModuleCat.zero_hom` as
  plan suggested) is the correct Mathlib lemma at
  `Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Basic` for `(0 : M ⟶ N).hom = 0`.

## Step 2 — `cechCofaceMap_pi_smul` L657 trailing sorry — PARTIAL (B1 only)

### Attempt 1 (B1 BRIDGE, COMMITTED)
- **Approach:** `simp_rw [← ModuleCat.piIsoPi_hom_ker_subtype_apply Z₂ j]`
  rewrites both `(Pi.π Z₂ j).hom z` occurrences to
  `(piIsoPi Z₂).hom.hom z j` form.
- **Result:** PARTIAL — committed at L656; goal advanced one step but the
  `(piIsoPi Z₂).hom.hom z j` form still has the OUTER `j`-eval and the
  INNER `((∑G) ≫ eqToHom).hom z` shape. The structural lemma's conclusion
  `e₂ ((∑F).hom z) = r • e₂ ((∑F).hom z')` is family-level (no `j`) and
  expects `(∑F).hom z` directly (no `≫ eqToHom`).

### Attempt 2 (suffices + congrFun + Pi.smul_apply, REVERTED)
- **Approach:** Hand-typed family-level `suffices h_fam : ... := by ...`
  with the literal alternating-sum body in the LHS/RHS of the family
  statement; then `simpa [Pi.smul_apply] using congrFun h_fam j`.
- **Result:** FAILED — `whnf` deterministic timeout (1.6M heartbeats) at
  L497 elaboration when the hand-typed literal triggered an instance-search
  cascade through `dif_pos hRel` / `ComplexShape.prev` reduction. Reverted.
- **Dead-end warning:** Hand-typing the alternating-sum literal in a
  `suffices` body re-introduces the iter-096 `whnf` timeout that defeated
  the refactor's `cechCofaceMap_pi_smul_summand` companion helper.

### Attempt 3 (refine alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux directly, FAILED)
- **Approach:** `refine congrFun (alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux Z₁ Z₂ Finset.univ ?F e₁ e₂ ?hF r y) j`
  to lift to family-level via `congrFun` and let Lean infer `?F` via
  Miller-pattern unification.
- **Result:** FAILED with two distinct errors:
  - First pass: `typeclass instance problem is stuck: Fintype ?m` —
    Lean cannot infer `Finset.univ`'s index type without an annotation.
  - Annotated as `(Finset.univ : Finset (Fin (n+1)))`: Type mismatch — the
    lemma's conclusion is `e₂ ((∑ i ∈ Finset.univ, ?F i).hom (e₁.symm (r•y))) j = (r • e₂ (...)) j`,
    but the goal's LHS is `(piIsoPi Z₂).hom.hom (((∑G_lit) ≫ eqToHom).hom z) j`.
    The `(∑F).hom z` vs `((∑G_lit) ≫ eqToHom).hom z` mismatch is not
    Miller-resolvable.

### Attempt 4 (rw [Preadditive.sum_comp] / rw [key₂] to push eqToHom inside, FAILED)
- **Approach:** Try `rw [Preadditive.sum_comp]` to convert `(∑G) ≫ E` to
  `∑(G_i ≫ E)`, putting the goal in the form the structural lemma expects.
- **Result:** FAILED on HOU. Pattern `(∑ j ∈ ?s, ?f j) ≫ ?g` does not
  match goal's `(∑ i, (-1)^↑i • Pi.lift (fun i_1 => ...uses i...)) ≫ eqToHom`
  because `?f i` cannot abstract the body's nested `i` references in
  non-Miller positions (`(-1)^↑i`, `(SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom`,
  multiple uses).
- Same HOU on `rw [key₂]` and `simp only [key₂]` — confirmed iter-094/095's
  HOU diagnosis transfers verbatim to `Preadditive.sum_comp`.

### Attempt 5 (refine with explicit F including eqToHom, FAILED)
- **Approach:** `have h_struct := alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux Z₁ Z₂ Finset.univ
  (fun i => (-1)^↑i • Pi.lift (...) ≫ eqToHom (...)) e₁ e₂`
  with literal `Pi.π Z₁ (...)` body.
- **Result:** FAILED with deep type mismatch:
  ```
  Pi.π (basicOpenCover ?m ∘ ?m_1) (...)  has type
    ∏ᶜ basicOpenCover ?m ∘ ?m_1 ⟶ ...
  but is expected to have type
    (∏ᶜ fun a ↦ basicOpenCover (↑s₀) (i_1 a)) ⟶ ...
  ```
  Even with eta-expanded form `fun a => basicOpenCover ↑s₀ (i_1 a)`, the
  inner index-type of the secondary `Pi.lift` (over
  `Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 1)` vs `Fin (n+1)`) doesn't unify.
- **Dead-end warning:** The eqToHom in the goal comes from `dif_pos hRel`
  bridging `(prev n + 1) → ↥s₀` and `n → ↥s₀` index types. Hand-providing
  F with eqToHom baked in re-triggers this bridge as a unification problem
  outside tactic state where `dif_pos hRel` cannot fire.

### Attempt 6 (rw [← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply], FAILED)
- **Approach:** Absorb outer `(piIsoPi Z₂).hom` into a categorical
  composition `(((∑G) ≫ eqToHom) ≫ piIsoPi Z₂.hom).hom z`.
- **Result:** FAILED — `(ConcreteCategory.hom ?g) ((ConcreteCategory.hom ?f) ?x)`
  pattern not found. Both outer and inner are in `ConcreteCategory.hom`
  form post-iter-095 cosmetic; the lemma matches only when `g.hom` and
  `f.hom` compose, not when both are in the unified form.

## Final state (verified)

- **Sorries:** 6 (down from 7). Active: L657 (cechCofaceMap_pi_smul Step 2
  bridging — partial B1 progress added), L749 (substep a), L1073 (outer
  scaffolding), L1101 (s₀-extra-degeneracy), L1291 (g_R.map_smul'), L1320
  (h_loc_exact). NB: `sorry`-token count includes prose/comment occurrences;
  actual unfilled `by` slots are 6.
- **L478 (alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux):** CLOSED with full proof body
  L478–L494.
- **L657 (cechCofaceMap_pi_smul trailing):** SORRY remains, with iter-097
  Step 2 (B1) bridging committed at L653–L656 (`simp_rw [← piIsoPi_hom_ker_subtype_apply Z₂ j]`).
- **File compiles:** `lean_diagnostic_messages` severity=error returns `[]`.
- **No new axioms.**
- **No `first | ... | sorry` wraps.**
- **Per-step LSP-verified.**

## Concrete next step for iter-098 (recommendation)

The plan-agent's iter-098 contingency was: **"escalate to focused follow-up
refactor adding `alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux_pi_proj` (specialisation of
`alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux` whose conclusion bakes in the outer `Pi.π Z₂ j`
projection AND the `eqToHom`-codomain bridge, matching L657's goal shape
directly)."** This iter-097 prover report fully confirms that recommendation.

The structural problem: the bridging chain
`(piIsoPi Z₂).hom.hom (((∑G_lit) ≫ eqToHom).hom z) j → e₂ ((∑F).hom z)`
requires either
(a) `Preadditive.sum_comp` HOU-rewrite (DEFEATED by iter-094/095/097), or
(b) explicit-F type ascription with literal Čech body (DEFEATED by
    `whnf` timeout / type-mismatch through `dif_pos hRel`).

The refactor approach: add a SECOND structural lemma whose conclusion
already bakes in `(Pi.π Z₂ j).hom (...) = r • (Pi.π Z₂ j).hom (...)` and
the `(∑G) ≫ eqToHom` composition. Then iter-099 prover's call site
reduces to per-summand R-linearity discharge (well-trodden iter-090/091
template using `Pi.lift_π_apply`, `Pi.smul_apply`, `map_mul`,
`presheafMap_restrict_collapse`).

Concrete suggested signature for iter-098 refactor:
```lean
theorem alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux_pi_proj
    {k : Type u} [Field k]
    {R : Type*} [Ring R]
    {ι₁ : Type u} {ι₂ : Type u} (Z₁ : ι₁ → ModuleCat.{u} k)
    (Z_intermediate : ι₂ → ModuleCat.{u} k)
    (Z₂ : ι₂ → ModuleCat.{u} k)
    [_mZ1 : Module R ((∀ i, Z₁ i))] [_mZ2 : Module R ((∀ j, Z₂ j))]
    {ι' : Type*} (s : Finset ι')
    (G : ι' → ((∏ᶜ Z₁ : ModuleCat.{u} k) ⟶ (∏ᶜ Z_intermediate : ModuleCat.{u} k)))
    (E : (∏ᶜ Z_intermediate : ModuleCat.{u} k) ⟶ (∏ᶜ Z₂ : ModuleCat.{u} k))
    (e₁ : (∏ᶜ Z₁ : ModuleCat.{u} k) ≃ₗ[k] ∀ i, Z₁ i)
    (e₂ : (∏ᶜ Z₂ : ModuleCat.{u} k) ≃ₗ[k] ∀ j, Z₂ j)
    (hG : ∀ i ∈ s, ∀ (r : R) (y : ∀ i, Z₁ i),
      e₂ ((G i ≫ E).hom (e₁.symm (r • y))) =
        r • e₂ ((G i ≫ E).hom (e₁.symm y))) (j : ι₂) :
    ∀ (r : R) (y : ∀ i, Z₁ i),
      (Pi.π Z₂ j).hom (((∑ i ∈ s, G i) ≫ E).hom (e₁.symm (r • y))) =
        r • (Pi.π Z₂ j).hom (((∑ i ∈ s, G i) ≫ E).hom (e₁.symm y))
```

Body: `Preadditive.sum_comp _ G E` rewrite + apply `alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux`
on `fun i => G i ≫ E`. Both rewrites are HOU-free at signature level
because G is a binder, not a literal. Iter-099 prover fills L657 with one
`exact` invocation.
